texto: She remembered the way the damp, coarse sand had clumped to her legs and hands, and burrowed beneath her nails and into the folds of her clothes, and she had wondered why the British children in her storybooks were always excited about going to the beach—just as now she wondered why the light from the lighthouse seemed to be coming from the landward side of the expressway. “I thought a lighthouse is out at sea.
explicacao: Citação de Yu-Mei Balasingamchow sobre temas ligados a familia. “She remembered the way the damp, coarse sand had clumped to her legs and hands, and burrowed bene…” articula familia, epigram-books, fiction em registro de familia, com relevância para contextos como fiction e lighthouse.

frase_original: She remembered the way the damp, coarse sand had clumped to her legs and hands, and burrowed beneath her nails and into the folds of her clothes, and she had wondered why the British children in her storybooks were always excited about going to the beach—just as now she wondered why the light from the lighthouse seemed to be coming from the landward side of the expressway. “I thought a lighthouse is out at sea.
